D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

Clip or Compress?

push a colour past the sRGB boundary and compare a hard clip to the edge against a soft compression that pulls the whole range inward

When a colour falls outside the destination gamut it must be moved in. Relative-colorimetric clipping snaps it to the nearest in-gamut chroma, leaving everything else untouched. Perceptual compression squeezes the whole chroma axis so relationships survive — at the cost of slightly desaturating in-gamut colours.

Set the lightness and hue, then drive the source chroma past the gamut boundary C_b. The plot marks the source, the clipped point (on the edge), and the compressed point (pulled in along the same hue). Two patches show the resulting colours.
